evidence.question_text_raw:
If your total monthly personal spending was 100 rupees, how would you divide it across the following? 
For example:
Gadgets / phone: 30-40%
Utilities: 20%
Food / outings 10-15%
Clothing / lifestyle 10%
Travel expesnse 5% (Travel with friends to northern)
Saving: 20-25%
Other expenses 
Probe:
Why did you allocate more/less to certain categories?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
I also use Facebook occasionally, mainly for short videos and general browsing, although TikTok remains my preferred platform for entertainment.
At this stage of my life, my income is still limited, so I have to be careful with my spending. Usually, I manage my expenses according to whatever amount I have available at the time. If there is something important that I really need, I may take support from my brother or family. For example, recently I needed a laptop, so I purchased an Infinix laptop with family support because it was necessary for my work and studies.
If I roughly break down my spending habits, around 30% of my expenses go towards food, while approximately 15% is spent on travel and transportation. I also try to save around 30% of my income whenever possible.
The remaining amount is generally spent on personal needs such as electronics, gadgets, and clothing. I do not shop for clothes very frequently; usually, I buy clothing once every 2–3 months or whenever there is a specific need. Overall, I try to maintain a balance between daily expenses, savings, and occasional purchases related to technology and personal use.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Tell me about your friend circle — what are they like? 
What kind of things do people in your circle talk about most? Gaming / gadgets / brands / money / lifestyle / fashion
What is your role in your friend circle e.g. influencial and leader, everyone listens to you and you make plans or you follow others plans?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
Since I work in a professional environment and have a personal interest in technology, I already have a reasonable amount of knowledge about laptops and electronics. However, before making any purchase, I still do some research to make sure I am making the right decision.
Usually, I start by watching a few videos online. Once I watch one or two videos about a specific laptop or device, TikTok's algorithm starts showing me many related videos automatically. This helps me gather different opinions, reviews, and user experiences.
If I want to compare specifications in more detail, I also use ChatGPT to understand differences between models, processors, RAM options, and overall value for money.
After doing online research, I usually visit Hafeez Center in person to see the product, compare options physically, and then make the final purchase decision. This is generally how I buy electronics — first online research through videos and AI tools, followed by a physical market visit before making the final choice.
I have around 5–6 close friends. Apart from them, I interact with many colleagues at the office because we spend a significant amount of time together in the firm. I also have some friends from my neighborhood whom I usually meet on weekends, along with a separate group of friends who play cricket.
My friends have different interests and hobbies. Some are interested in watches, while others are passionate about premium smartphones, especially iPhones and other flagship devices.
If my friends were asked to describe me, they would probably say that I am a very busy person. The main reason is my CA field, which is known for being demanding and time-consuming. Even now, although I am slightly freer than before, most of my time is still occupied by work and studies.
For example, when I recently wanted to buy a laptop, I discussed it with my office colleagues and took their advice before making the purchase decision.
Among our friend circle, there is usually one person who takes the initiative and creates plans. Most outings, gatherings, or meetups start from his side. Because of my busy schedule, I am rarely the one who initiates plans myself. However, whenever a plan is made and my schedule allows, I make sure to join.
The reason I usually avoid planning events myself is that arranging everything requires time and coordination, and due to my professional commitments, I often cannot guarantee my availability. Therefore, I prefer joining plans rather than organizing them.
evidence.answer_text_clean:
Since I work in a professional environment and have a personal interest in technology, I already have a reasonable amount of knowledge about laptops and electronics. However, before making any purchase, I still do some research to make sure I am making the right decision.
Usually, I start by watching a few videos online. Once I watch one or two videos about a specific laptop or device, TikTok's algorithm starts showing me many related videos automatically. This helps me gather different opinions, reviews, and user experiences.
If I want to compare specifications in more detail, I also use ChatGPT to understand differences between models, processors, RAM options, and overall value for money.
After doing online research, I usually visit Hafeez Center in person to see the product, compare options physically, and then make the final purchase decision. This is generally how I buy electronics — first online research through videos and AI tools, followed by a physical market visit before making the final choice.
I have around 5–6 close friends. Apart from them, I interact with many colleagues at the office because we spend a significant amount of time together in the firm. I also have some friends from my neighborhood whom I usually meet on weekends, along with a separate group of friends who play cricket.
My friends have different interests and hobbies. Some are interested in watches, while others are passionate about premium smartphones, especially iPhones and other flagship devices.
If my friends were asked to describe me, they would probably say that I am a very busy person. The main reason is my CA field, which is known for being demanding and time-consuming. Even now, although I am slightly freer than before, most of my time is still occupied by work and studies.
For example, when I recently wanted to buy a laptop, I discussed it with my office colleagues and took their advice before making the purchase decision.
Among our friend circle, there is usually one person who takes the initiative and creates plans. Most outings, gatherings, or meetups start from his side. Because of my busy schedule, I am rarely the one who initiates plans myself. However, whenever a plan is made and my schedule allows, I make sure to join.
The reason I usually avoid planning events myself is that arranging everything requires time and coordination, and due to my professional commitments, I often cannot guarantee my availability. Therefore, I prefer joining plans rather than organizing them.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Do you feel your phone represents you as a person? How? What kind of phone gives a “strong impression” in your circle? 
 Probe: Expensive vs performance vs brand image perception 
Is the phone a status symbol or basic need in your life?
Do people notice or comment on your phone?
If one day you wake up and there is no phones in the world then how would you feel and react?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
For me, a phone has become an extremely important part of daily life. If I try to imagine life without a phone, it feels like going 20–30 years back in time. It would be like people sitting outside in streets and neighborhoods on charpoys with very limited connectivity and communication.
I feel that if phones did not exist, a major part of modern connectivity would disappear. Today, smartphones are not just communication devices; they have become a central part of how people stay connected, informed, and engaged with the world.
I also think phones have become a part of social identity. The type of phone a person carries often influences how others perceive them. A good phone helps in many practical situations, especially during gatherings and social events where people frequently take photos and videos.
In my opinion, smartphones have also become a status symbol to some extent. In social gatherings, people often notice what device others are using. Sometimes there is a perception that if someone is carrying a very old or basic phone, it may reflect their financial situation, whether that assumption is correct or not.
Because of this, factors such as phone design, overall look and feel, memory capacity, RAM, and specifications matter a lot to people today. Beyond functionality, the appearance and image associated with a smartphone have become increasingly important in influencing purchase decisions and personal satisfaction.

evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I looked at the specifications, I liked several things about the phone, particularly the processor, design, and battery. My previous Vivo device had become quite slow, and since it was also a second-hand phone that had become old, I decided it was time to change it.
The first time I noticed Infinix Hot 60 was through a Facebook advertisement. Initially, I was actually looking at Samsung devices within the same budget range. However, when I compared the options, I felt that Infinix was offering much stronger specifications for the price.
The processor seemed better, the design looked attractive, the camera appeared promising, and overall the phone felt like a stronger package. Since I am particularly conscious about phone speed and performance, my primary focus was on getting a device that felt fast and responsive. Because of that, the processor became one of the biggest deciding factors for me.
When I visited the market, I also checked the dummy unit and was satisfied with the camera and overall design.
After seeing the Facebook advertisement, I searched for the phone on Google, visited the official Infinix Pakistan website, reviewed the specifications in more detail, and watched several videos and reviews online. After completing my research, I decided to purchase the phone.

Can you please quickly walk me through how you use your smartphone from morning till night? (Moderator: Try to get and establish top 3 specific usage categories based on user's phone usage)
1. Communication & Social Connection
How frequently do you connect with your friends and family using your smartphone?
How do you typically communicate with friends, family, or colleagues using your smartphone?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Do you prefer voice calls, messaging, or video chats?
Any challenges that you face during communication? Probe in detail
2. Entertainment

3. Work & Study

4. Shopping, Online Services & Transactions
Using your phone for online shopping, mobile banking, or paying bills?

5. Camera Usage (Photos, Videos & Creativity)

Moderator to confirm & assure: Are there any other major usages?

evidence.answer_text_raw:
I finally selected this phone because of its battery, RAM, and camera. These were the three main factors that influenced my decision.
 Another important reason was that my friends strongly recommended the device. They told me that if I bought this phone, I would not regret the decision later. Personally, my friends are usually my most trusted source when it comes to phone recommendations because they share real-life experiences rather than promotional information.
 Apart from that, I also checked online reviews and videos. Those reviews helped me understand the strengths and weaknesses of the device and gave me additional confidence about the purchase.
 When I visited the shop, the shopkeeper also spent quite a bit of time explaining the phone's features and benefits. After hearing recommendations from friends, reviewing information online, and getting further reassurance from the shopkeeper, I felt confident enough to finalize the purchase and buy the phone.
One thing I really liked about this phone was its charging speed. Compared to my previous device, charging felt much faster and more convenient.
To purchase this phone, I sold my old phone and also saved money myself. I managed to save around 50,000 PKR over a period of approximately two months before making the purchase.
Another positive aspect was that the charger was included in the box. I consider that a very important benefit because many brands are now reducing accessories in the package. Having a charger included added value to the overall purchase experience.
The phone also comes with an in-display fingerprint sensor, which I find convenient and modern.
If I think about future improvements, I believe the device could be made even better by offering 5G connectivity instead of 4G, upgrading the processor further, and improving the camera quality.
In terms of app usage, the two applications that are most commonly running on my phone are WhatsApp and TikTok. Sometimes it is WhatsApp and ChatGPT instead, depending on what I am doing. These are the applications I use most frequently throughout the day for communication, entertainment, and information.
I mainly use TikTok and Facebook for entertainment and content consumption. For communication, WhatsApp is my primary application, along with normal phone calls whenever needed.
For watching movies and shows, I use Netflix, and most of the content I watch is in English.
In terms of gaming, I mainly play PUBG: Battlegrounds and Ludo Star. Occasionally, I experience slow performance in PUBG, but in most cases, it is due to internet connectivity issues rather than the phone itself. The same applies to Ludo Star, where gameplay quality largely depends on network stability. Once the internet connection improves, the issue is usually resolved.
Previously, I used to spend around 3–4 hours playing games, but now my gaming time has reduced significantly.
For work-related activities, the applications I use most are ChatGPT and WhatsApp. These two apps are essential for communication, information gathering, and managing work-related tasks.
As for the camera, I do not use it very frequently in daily life. Most of my camera usage happens during social gatherings, outings, or when I am spending time with friends. On average, I would say I use the camera meaningfully once or twice every couple of weeks, mainly for group photos, memories, and social occasions rather than regular daily use.
For financial transactions, I regularly use MCB Live and Easypaisa. These applications are part of my daily routine and are used frequently for payments, transfers, and other financial activities.
From my experience, I have not faced any major issues from the phone side while using banking or payment applications. Most of the problems, if they occur, are usually related to internet connectivity rather than the device itself. As long as the network connection is stable, these applications work smoothly for me.
In terms of design, I am generally satisfied with the phone. The only thing I would slightly change is the camera module because I feel it protrudes a bit more than necessary from the back of the device. Other than that, I think the overall design, appearance, and build are quite good.

evidence.question_text_raw: Ovelall Feedback on phone
evidence.answer_text_raw:
The phone immediately gives the impression of being a premium and expensive device. It does not feel cheap or lightweight, and the overall build quality feels solid. The brightness looks good, the battery appears strong, and the processor also seems capable based on the hands-on experience. One thing I particularly noticed was that even after keeping the camera open for some time, I did not see any obvious battery drop, which created a positive impression regarding battery efficiency.
The design is one of the strongest aspects of the device. 
I really like the camera module design, the unique color, and especially the arrow/matrix pattern on the back panel. The color feels different from what is commonly available in the market and makes the phone stand out. Overall, the design looks modern, attractive, and eye-catching.
Based on the hands-on experience, I would be willing to pay around PKR 65,000–70,000 for this phone. If the actual price were around PKR 80,000, I would first want to evaluate the specifications more carefully before making a decision. At that price point, I would compare the battery, processor, camera quality, RAM and storage configuration, warranty coverage, and 5G support against other devices available in the market.
For me, a phone in the 80,000 PKR segment should offer a strong processor, good battery life, 8GB RAM with 256GB storage, a better camera than competing devices, and reliable after-sales support. Warranty becomes very important at that price level. If the phone came with a two-year warranty, device insurance, and strong after-sales support, I would be comfortable paying even slightly more.
One feature that would attract me the most is a trade-in program where I could exchange my old phone for a new device. That would be a very strong purchase motivator.
Overall, the design, unique color, camera appearance, battery perception, and premium feel are the biggest reasons why the device stands out to me. Even if another brand offered similar specifications at a slightly lower price, I would still lean towards Infinix because I already have confidence in the brand and feel more comfortable purchasing it.

evidence.answer_text_raw:
For entertainment, I spend most of my time on TikTok. I am a big fan of Babar Azam, so I regularly watch cricket-related content, including match highlights, player discussions, analysis videos, and cricket reels. Apart from cricket, I also watch trending content, entertainment videos, and topics related to current events. Whatever is trending at the time—whether it is cricket matches, Eid celebrations, Qurbani-related content, or other viral topics—I usually follow it through social media.
On weekends, my routine is much more relaxed. I usually wake up around 10:00 AM or 11:00 AM, have breakfast, and help with any household work if needed. Because of the extreme heat, I prefer staying indoors for most of the day and usually remain at home until around 5:00 PM. During this time, I spend a significant amount of time on my phone, mostly watching TikTok videos, cricket-related content, Facebook videos, and other entertainment content. In the evening, I may go out with friends or family, but overall, weekends are mainly spent relaxing and using my phone for entertainment.
In my free time, I usually spend time with friends. Sometimes we play PUBG, and other times we play Ludo or other casual games together.
When it comes to food, I generally prefer traditional desi food. If I want desi food, I usually eat somewhere close to home. However, when I am in the mood for fast food, I often go towards Gulberg with friends.
Cricket is also one of the activities we regularly enjoy. Usually, around 10 friends gather to play. By the time we finish the match and get free, it is often quite late, sometimes around 2 AM. After that, we usually stop somewhere for food before heading home.
Apart from cricket, these days we have also started visiting swimming pools quite frequently because of the summer season. It has become one of our preferred ways of spending time with friends and staying active during the hot weather.

What are the additional benefits attract you when you buy a product e.g. discounts, gifts, memberships, collaboration with other brands/ products etc.

evidence.answer_text_raw:
The brand name plays a very important role in the purchase decision. Before buying a phone, people often consider the reputation and image of the brand because it creates confidence regarding quality, performance, and reliability.
I also think bundled gifts and accessories matter a lot. Things like a charger, handfree, protective accessories, or other value-added items can influence a person's decision because they increase the overall value of the purchase.
For online purchases, I feel free delivery is very important. If customers are already spending a significant amount on a smartphone, then delivery charges can become an unnecessary frustration and may negatively affect the buying experience.
In terms of marketing, I believe smartphone companies should invest more in visible branding and advertising. They should focus on locations where people frequently visit and spend time.
For example, branding can be placed at major retail shops, food outlets, and popular gathering spots. Places such as Ranchers, Cheezious, and popular clothing stores receive a large number of visitors daily, making them effective locations for brand visibility.
I also think branding near busy commercial areas, government offices, and high-footfall locations can help increase awareness. However, those locations may also have stronger competition from other brands, which is why companies should maintain visibility both in premium retail markets and popular lifestyle locations where their target audience regularly visits.

For online purchases, I feel free delivery is very important. If customers are already spending a significant amount on a smartphone, then delivery charges can become an unnecessary frustration and may negatively affect the buying experience.
In terms of marketing, I believe smartphone companies should invest more in visible branding and advertising. They should focus on locations where people frequently visit and spend time.
For example, branding can be placed at major retail shops, food outlets, and popular gathering spots. Places such as Ranchers, Cheezious, and popular clothing stores receive a large number of visitors daily, making them effective locations for brand visibility.
I also think branding near busy commercial areas, government offices, and high-footfall locations can help increase awareness. However, those locations may also have stronger competition from other brands, which is why companies should maintain visibility both in premium retail markets and popular lifestyle locations where their target audience regularly visits.

evidence.answer_text_raw:

I would also expect further improvements in the camera. My main expectation is that pictures should be sharper, with better pixel clarity and overall image quality compared to current devices.
In terms of budget, I would be comfortable spending between 55,000 PKR and 70,000 PKR. Ideally, I would prefer something within that range rather than moving into a much higher price segment.
If Infinix launches a newer model within this budget that offers better specifications, especially in terms of camera quality, 5G support, RAM, and storage, I would seriously consider purchasing that device as my next phone.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Are there any brands you dislike and would never consider buying? What's your impression of disliked brands? 
Also Impressions on the Brand List: Apple, Samsung, Xiaomi, Redmi, OPPO, POCO, vivo, Realme, Huawei, TECNO, Infinix.
evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I think about different smartphone brands, certain perceptions automatically come to mind based on my own experience and what I observe around me.
For Apple, the impression is that it is a brand for people who are very brand-conscious. Even older iPhones continue to be used for years, which shows strong customer loyalty. People who buy Apple usually stay within the Apple ecosystem and continue upgrading to newer iPhones.
When I hear Samsung, the first thing that comes to mind is that it is an expensive brand. The perception is generally premium, but also costly.
For Redmi, I honestly do not have a very strong positive impression. When I hear the brand name, it does not immediately make me think of a particularly good or desirable phone.
For Oppo, my impression is generally positive. I feel it is a good brand with decent devices and a respectable market image.
For Vivo, I am personally not very interested. Whenever I think about Vivo, I am uncertain about long-term performance and reliability. The thought that comes to mind is whether it will perform well over time or not.
For Realme, I do not immediately associate it with high-quality devices. It does not have a particularly strong image in my mind.
For Tecno, the main concern that comes to mind is resale value. I do not feel that Tecno devices retain their value as strongly as some competing brands.
For Infinix, my perception is quite positive. I think it offers good value for money, and I also believe its resale value is relatively good. Because of that, it remains one of the brands I would consider again in the future.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
How would you describe your spending philosophy? 
(Spontaneous vs. Planned, Advance Consumption/loans vs. Living within One's Means, Buy What You Like vs. Careful Selection) Can you give an example? 
How do you usually spend your personal income or pocket money in a typical month?
Probes: What are your main spending categories? (e.g., food, outings, gadgets, gaming, clothing, etc.)
How important is spending on gadgets or your phone compared to other things? Do you plan your spending or is it more spontaneous? 
How do you usually spend money/pocket money?
Do you generally try to buy and spend on new or innovative products first in the market or do you wait and first learn from others experiences or wait for discounts?
1) If you were to describe your personality when it comes to spending money on things in three words, how would you describe it?
2) When it comes to buying mobile phone, which type do you think you aare more like: such as 1) Peopler who are willing to spend a little mmore for what they like 2) People who are willing to spend cost-effectiveness in everything 3)People who pursue novelty and enjoy trying new things 4) People who follow those around them/word of mouth
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
For entertainment, I spend most of my time on TikTok. I am a big fan of Babar Azam, so I regularly watch cricket-related content, including match highlights, player discussions, analysis videos, and cricket reels. Apart from cricket, I also watch trending content, entertainment videos, and topics related to current events. Whatever is trending at the time—whether it is cricket matches, Eid celebrations, Qurbani-related content, or other viral topics—I usually follow it through social media.
On weekends, my routine is much more relaxed. I usually wake up around 10:00 AM or 11:00 AM, have breakfast, and help with any household work if needed. Because of the extreme heat, I prefer staying indoors for most of the day and usually remain at home until around 5:00 PM. During this time, I spend a significant amount of time on my phone, mostly watching TikTok videos, cricket-related content, Facebook videos, and other entertainment content. In the evening, I may go out with friends or family, but overall, weekends are mainly spent relaxing and using my phone for entertainment.
In my free time, I usually spend time with friends. Sometimes we play PUBG, and other times we play Ludo or other casual games together.
When it comes to food, I generally prefer traditional desi food. If I want desi food, I usually eat somewhere close to home. However, when I am in the mood for fast food, I often go towards Gulberg with friends.
Cricket is also one of the activities we regularly enjoy. Usually, around 10 friends gather to play. By the time we finish the match and get free, it is often quite late, sometimes around 2 AM. After that, we usually stop somewhere for food before heading home.
Apart from cricket, these days we have also started visiting swimming pools quite frequently because of the summer season. It has become one of our preferred ways of spending time with friends and staying active during the hot weather.

Gadgets / phone: 30-40%
Utilities: 20%
Food / outings 10-15%
Clothing / lifestyle 10%
Travel expesnse 5% (Travel with friends to northern)
Saving: 20-25%
Other expenses

all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
I also use Facebook occasionally, mainly for short videos and general browsing, although TikTok remains my preferred platform for entertainment.
At this stage of my life, my income is still limited, so I have to be careful with my spending. Usually, I manage my expenses according to whatever amount I have available at the time. If there is something important that I really need, I may take support from my brother or family. For example, recently I needed a laptop, so I purchased an Infinix laptop with family support because it was necessary for my work and studies.
If I roughly break down my spending habits, around 30% of my expenses go towards food, while approximately 15% is spent on travel and transportation. I also try to save around 30% of my income whenever possible.
The remaining amount is generally spent on personal needs such as electronics, gadgets, and clothing. I do not shop for clothes very frequently; usually, I buy clothing once every 2–3 months or whenever there is a specific need. Overall, I try to maintain a balance between daily expenses, savings, and occasional purchases related to technology and personal use.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Tell me about your friend circle — what are they like? 
What kind of things do people in your circle talk about most? Gaming / gadgets / brands / money / lifestyle / fashion
What is your role in your friend circle e.g. influencial and leader, everyone listens to you and you make plans or you follow others plans?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
Since I work in a professional environment and have a personal interest in technology, I already have a reasonable amount of knowledge about laptops and electronics. However, before making any purchase, I still do some research to make sure I am making the right decision.
Usually, I start by watching a few videos online. Once I watch one or two videos about a specific laptop or device, TikTok's algorithm starts showing me many related videos automatically. This helps me gather different opinions, reviews, and user experiences.
If I want to compare specifications in more detail, I also use ChatGPT to understand differences between models, processors, RAM options, and overall value for money.
After doing online research, I usually visit Hafeez Center in person to see the product, compare options physically, and then make the final purchase decision. This is generally how I buy electronics — first online research through videos and AI tools, followed by a physical market visit before making the final choice.
I have around 5–6 close friends. Apart from them, I interact with many colleagues at the office because we spend a significant amount of time together in the firm. I also have some friends from my neighborhood whom I usually meet on weekends, along with a separate group of friends who play cricket.
My friends have different interests and hobbies. Some are interested in watches, while others are passionate about premium smartphones, especially iPhones and other flagship devices.
If my friends were asked to describe me, they would probably say that I am a very busy person. The main reason is my CA field, which is known for being demanding and time-consuming. Even now, although I am slightly freer than before, most of my time is still occupied by work and studies.
For example, when I recently wanted to buy a laptop, I discussed it with my office colleagues and took their advice before making the purchase decision.
Among our friend circle, there is usually one person who takes the initiative and creates plans. Most outings, gatherings, or meetups start from his side. Because of my busy schedule, I am rarely the one who initiates plans myself. However, whenever a plan is made and my schedule allows, I make sure to join.
The reason I usually avoid planning events myself is that arranging everything requires time and coordination, and due to my professional commitments, I often cannot guarantee my availability. Therefore, I prefer joining plans rather than organizing them.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Do you feel your phone represents you as a person? How? What kind of phone gives a “strong impression” in your circle? 
 Probe: Expensive vs performance vs brand image perception 
Is the phone a status symbol or basic need in your life?
Do people notice or comment on your phone?
If one day you wake up and there is no phones in the world then how would you feel and react?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
For me, a phone has become an extremely important part of daily life. If I try to imagine life without a phone, it feels like going 20–30 years back in time. It would be like people sitting outside in streets and neighborhoods on charpoys with very limited connectivity and communication.
I feel that if phones did not exist, a major part of modern connectivity would disappear. Today, smartphones are not just communication devices; they have become a central part of how people stay connected, informed, and engaged with the world.
I also think phones have become a part of social identity. The type of phone a person carries often influences how others perceive them. A good phone helps in many practical situations, especially during gatherings and social events where people frequently take photos and videos.
In my opinion, smartphones have also become a status symbol to some extent. In social gatherings, people often notice what device others are using. Sometimes there is a perception that if someone is carrying a very old or basic phone, it may reflect their financial situation, whether that assumption is correct or not.
Because of this, factors such as phone design, overall look and feel, memory capacity, RAM, and specifications matter a lot to people today. Beyond functionality, the appearance and image associated with a smartphone have become increasingly important in influencing purchase decisions and personal satisfaction.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
What are the additional benefits attract you when you buy a product e.g. discounts, gifts, memberships, collaboration with other brands/ products etc.
What type of discounts, gifts or memberships do you expect that will excite you and why?
Considering your situation, with which brands or products a tech product or phone should collaborate that will exite you?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
The brand name plays a very important role in the purchase decision. Before buying a phone, people often consider the reputation and image of the brand because it creates confidence regarding quality, performance, and reliability.
I also think bundled gifts and accessories matter a lot. Things like a charger, handfree, protective accessories, or other value-added items can influence a person's decision because they increase the overall value of the purchase.
For online purchases, I feel free delivery is very important. If customers are already spending a significant amount on a smartphone, then delivery charges can become an unnecessary frustration and may negatively affect the buying experience.
In terms of marketing, I believe smartphone companies should invest more in visible branding and advertising. They should focus on locations where people frequently visit and spend time.
For example, branding can be placed at major retail shops, food outlets, and popular gathering spots. Places such as Ranchers, Cheezious, and popular clothing stores receive a large number of visitors daily, making them effective locations for brand visibility.
I also think branding near busy commercial areas, government offices, and high-footfall locations can help increase awareness. However, those locations may also have stronger competition from other brands, which is why companies should maintain visibility both in premium retail markets and popular lifestyle locations where their target audience regularly visits.

all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I looked at the specifications, I liked several things about the phone, particularly the processor, design, and battery. My previous Vivo device had become quite slow, and since it was also a second-hand phone that had become old, I decided it was time to change it.
The first time I noticed Infinix Hot 60 was through a Facebook advertisement. Initially, I was actually looking at Samsung devices within the same budget range. However, when I compared the options, I felt that Infinix was offering much stronger specifications for the price.
The processor seemed better, the design looked attractive, the camera appeared promising, and overall the phone felt like a stronger package. Since I am particularly conscious about phone speed and performance, my primary focus was on getting a device that felt fast and responsive. Because of that, the processor became one of the biggest deciding factors for me.
When I visited the market, I also checked the dummy unit and was satisfied with the camera and overall design.
After seeing the Facebook advertisement, I searched for the phone on Google, visited the official Infinix Pakistan website, reviewed the specifications in more detail, and watched several videos and reviews online. After completing my research, I decided to purchase the phone.

Can you please quickly walk me through how you use your smartphone from morning till night? (Moderator: Try to get and establish top 3 specific usage categories based on user's phone usage)
1. Communication & Social Connection
How frequently do you connect with your friends and family using your smartphone?
How do you typically communicate with friends, family, or colleagues using your smartphone?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Do you prefer voice calls, messaging, or video chats?
Any challenges that you face during communication? Probe in detail
2. Entertainment
What do you usually do for entertainment on your smartphone? e.g. watch videos, scroll social media, play games, or listen to music?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Any kind of difficulties that you face while using entertaining apps?
 
3. Work & Study
Are you using your smartphone for any work- or study-related tasks?
What kind of apps or tools do you use?
How does it help you with productivity or learning?
Are there any challenges you face when using a smartphone for work or education?
4. Shopping, Online Services & Transactions
Using your phone for online shopping, mobile banking, or paying bills?
What was the experience like? 
In what situations do you prefer using your phone over going physically to a bank/shop?
5. Camera Usage (Photos, Videos & Creativity)
How often do you take photos or make videos with your phone? 
What kind of edits you apply or share photos/videos? If so, how and with which apps?
Moderator to confirm & assure: Are there any other major usages?

all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
I finally selected this phone because of its battery, RAM, and camera. These were the three main factors that influenced my decision.
 Another important reason was that my friends strongly recommended the device. They told me that if I bought this phone, I would not regret the decision later. Personally, my friends are usually my most trusted source when it comes to phone recommendations because they share real-life experiences rather than promotional information.
 Apart from that, I also checked online reviews and videos. Those reviews helped me understand the strengths and weaknesses of the device and gave me additional confidence about the purchase.
 When I visited the shop, the shopkeeper also spent quite a bit of time explaining the phone's features and benefits. After hearing recommendations from friends, reviewing information online, and getting further reassurance from the shopkeeper, I felt confident enough to finalize the purchase and buy the phone.
One thing I really liked about this phone was its charging speed. Compared to my previous device, charging felt much faster and more convenient.
To purchase this phone, I sold my old phone and also saved money myself. I managed to save around 50,000 PKR over a period of approximately two months before making the purchase.

If Infinix launches a newer model within this budget that offers better specifications, especially in terms of camera quality, 5G support, RAM, and storage, I would seriously consider purchasing that device as my next phone.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Are there any brands you dislike and would never consider buying? What's your impression of disliked brands? 
Also Impressions on the Brand List: Apple, Samsung, Xiaomi, Redmi, OPPO, POCO, vivo, Realme, Huawei, TECNO, Infinix.
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I think about different smartphone brands, certain perceptions automatically come to mind based on my own experience and what I observe around me.
For Apple, the impression is that it is a brand for people who are very brand-conscious. Even older iPhones continue to be used for years, which shows strong customer loyalty. People who buy Apple usually stay within the Apple ecosystem and continue upgrading to newer iPhones.
When I hear Samsung, the first thing that comes to mind is that it is an expensive brand. The perception is generally premium, but also costly.
For Redmi, I honestly do not have a very strong positive impression. When I hear the brand name, it does not immediately make me think of a particularly good or desirable phone.
For Oppo, my impression is generally positive. I feel it is a good brand with decent devices and a respectable market image.
For Vivo, I am personally not very interested. Whenever I think about Vivo, I am uncertain about long-term performance and reliability. The thought that comes to mind is whether it will perform well over time or not.
For Realme, I do not immediately associate it with high-quality devices. It does not have a particularly strong image in my mind.
For Tecno, the main concern that comes to mind is resale value. I do not feel that Tecno devices retain their value as strongly as some competing brands.
For Infinix, my perception is quite positive. I think it offers good value for money, and I also believe its resale value is relatively good. Because of that, it remains one of the brands I would consider again in the future.

all_evidence.question_text_raw: Ovelall Feedback on phone
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
The phone immediately gives the impression of being a premium and expensive device. It does not feel cheap or lightweight, and the overall build quality feels solid. The brightness looks good, the battery appears strong, and the processor also seems capable based on the hands-on experience. One thing I particularly noticed was that even after keeping the camera open for some time, I did not see any obvious battery drop, which created a positive impression regarding battery efficiency.
The design is one of the strongest aspects of the device. 
I really like the camera module design, the unique color, and especially the arrow/matrix pattern on the back panel. The color feels different from what is commonly available in the market and makes the phone stand out. Overall, the design looks modern, attractive, and eye-catching.
Based on the hands-on experience, I would be willing to pay around PKR 65,000–70,000 for this phone. If the actual price were around PKR 80,000, I would first want to evaluate the specifications more carefully before making a decision. At that price point, I would compare the battery, processor, camera quality, RAM and storage configuration, warranty coverage, and 5G support against other devices available in the market.
For me, a phone in the 80,000 PKR segment should offer a strong processor, good battery life, 8GB RAM with 256GB storage, a better camera than competing devices, and reliable after-sales support. Warranty becomes very important at that price level. If the phone came with a two-year warranty, device insurance, and strong after-sales support, I would be comfortable paying even slightly more.
One feature that would attract me the most is a trade-in program where I could exchange my old phone for a new device. That would be a very strong purchase motivator.
Overall, the design, unique color, camera appearance, battery perception, and premium feel are the biggest reasons why the device stands out to me. Even if another brand offered similar specifications at a slightly lower price, I would still lean towards Infinix because I already have confidence in the brand and feel more comfortable purchasing it.

rows.question_text_raw:
- Let's talk about the phones you have owned. Tell me about your last three smartphones — starting from the one before your current phone, going back.
▸ Device 1 (most recent before current): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year purchased?
▸ Device 2: Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
▸ Device 3 (oldest): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
- Looking at these three phones — how often have you been changing your phone, roughly?▸ Every 1 year? 2 years? 3+ years?▸ Was the replacement planned or forced (broken, stolen, lost)?

- We just know about your privious devices. Generally under what circumstances do you usually choose to replace your phone with a new one? 
- What were the reasons that drove you to change phones for each of your previous phones?
- When did you start considering buying a new phone (before you purchased the current one)? Why did you start considering at that time?

- Take me back to the time when you first started thinking about changing your previous phone. What happened?
- How long before buying did you start thinking about it?
- [Pain Point ] Is there pain points with the previous phone? [IF YES]
- What were the main problems or frustrations with your previous device that pushed you to change?
▸ Battery? Performance? Camera? Storage? Software?
▸ Display issues? Heating? Hanging or crashing?
▸ Were these issues there from the start, or did they develop over time?

Before you started looking, what were the specific things you wanted in your new phone?
▸ Must-have features — what were the top 3?
▸ Any features you had heard about and wanted to try?
▸ Were there any deal-breakers — things you absolutely did not want?
- What are the reasons for considering such specific requirements? How did you evaluate and measure your needs and the functions that the product can provide?

- [Circle Influence] Who influenced you to be interested in it? Through what channels did you learn about it? What aspect attracted you? Do you still remember the specific content that influenced you at that time?

- Did you have a budget before purchase? What was the budget range? How was this budget formed? What were the upper and lower limits of the budget respectively? Did the budget increase or decrease compared with previous phone? Why?
- [For users who upgraded budget] What are the reason for the upgrading and how did you consider the significant increase in budget compared to the previous one?
- [For users who downgraded budget] What are the reason for the downgrading and how did you consider the significant decrease in budget compared to the previous one?

- [For brands the candidate are aware of] How did you come into contact with these brands？What information can help you make a comparison? After receiving this message, why would you approve? [Note: verify whether candidate only considers brands that has been used and he/she is familiar with (small-scale screening), or considers 1-2 brands within the target range (medium-scale), or considers a wide range (including all mainstream brands in the market)]
- Where do you usually prefer to learn about relevant information on mobile phones before purchasing one?

- Why do you choose these channels to learn about mobile phone information? What are the differences between different channels? [Note: Dig deep into how channel awareness is formed] If the information from different channels varies, which channel's information feedback will you prioritize?

- Among your colleagues, friends, and family members around you, what brands of mobile phone do they use? Is there someone you trust his opinion about mobile phones? Why do you trust his opinion? How did they recommend it to you? Why can this point impress you?

- What is the order of your information search? How do you use different channels (such as Google, social media, etc.)? [Note: Explore the formation of differentiated positioning and usage of channels, and the reasons behind. For example, some channel is used for avoiding pitfalls screening and some is for price comparison]

- [Self-initiated online search] Which platform is mainly used for searching? Why choose this platform? What keywords will be searched? What are the impressions of Google, YT...? Which platform is most trusted, and why?

- [Self-experience offline] Which aspect attracted you during the introduction at offline stores or by sales promoters? [For students] What offline activities are you interested in? What activities on campus can students actively participate in?

- [Passive Information Reach] Offline big brand advertisements, online official websites, press conferences (watch LIVE/ read text and image summaries), video websites (Ins, YT, etc.), e-commerce websites (advertising recommendations/user reviews), tech bloggers, Google, professional website reviews, variety shows, shopping guide Moments, etc.
- After learning about this/these phone(s), have you continued to use any channels to further understand these phones? (For example, through what keywords and on which platforms did you search?)
- After the search is completed, what information (brand/model/unique selling point/review) caught your attention? What specifically do you remember? Why were you attracted?

- When you had your shortlist, how did you make the final call? Walk me through the thinking.
▸ Did you start with a brand preference, then check price and specs?
▸ Did you fix a budget first, then find the best specs within it?
▸ Did a specific feature or specification tip the decision?

If you had to rank the three factors — Brand, Price, and Specifications — in the order they influenced your final purchase, what would that order be? Why?
▸ Was there a moment of doubt before you pressed 'buy' or handed over the cash?
▸ Did anyone else influence your final decision — friend, family, shopkeeper?

- What are the things you genuinely love about this phone?
▸ Camera quality, gaming performance, battery, design, display?
▸ Software features — AI features, UI smoothness?
▸ Value for money — do you feel you got a good deal?

- What are the things that bother you or disappoint you about this phone?
▸ Any features that don't work as advertised?
▸ Any performance issues that have developed over time?
▸ Anything that your friends' phones do better?

[Pain Points] - Have you faced any specific issues — heating, battery drain, camera quality in low light, software bugs?
▸ How frequently do these happen?
▸ Have you approached customer service or a repair shop?
▸ How was that experience?

- How satisfied are you with your current phone? If the full score for satisfaction is 10 points, what is your current satisfaction score? Why is it this score, and specifically, what are the aspects you are satisfied and dissatisfied with?

- Have you ever recommended your phone to others? To whom did you recommend it? Through what form? How did you recommend it at that time? What was the content of the recommendation? What was the reaction of others? Did any purchase behavior occur?

- When do you think you will next change your phone?
Let the respondent answer freely first. Then probe each layer below separately.
▸ Are you already thinking about it actively, or is it still vague?
▸ Any specific trigger that would make you change sooner — a new launch, a problem developing?
▸ Has anything recently made you look at another phone and think 'I want that'?
▸ Student: Will a life event trigger it — new semester, graduation, internship, getting a job?
▸ Professional: Would a promotion, new job, or salary hike push the decision?
▸ Is there a specific season or time of year when you prefer to buy — Eid, year-end sale, back-to-school?

- What would your next phone need to offer that your current one doesn't?
- Any specific features you have seen advertised or on a friend's phone that you want?
Moderator: Use the feature prompts below only if not already mentioned.
▸ Camera — is camera quality a must-have or just nice to have for you? Why?
– Main camera MP, night mode, portrait / bokeh, video quality, front camera for selfies?
▸ Battery — what is your minimum expectation for battery life in hours? Fast charging?
– Have you ever had a phone where battery ruined your day? Tell me about that.
▸ Performance & speed — gaming, multitasking, apps loading?
– Do you feel your current phone slows down? When does that frustrate you most?
▸ Display — size, resolution, refresh rate, brightness in sunlight?
– Have you used a phone with a noticeably better screen than yours? How did it feel?
▸ Storage — how much do you need? Do you use cloud or prefer local storage?
▸ RAM — do you know how much RAM your current phone has? Does it matter to you?
▸ Design & build — does the look and feel of the phone matter? Premium feel?
– Would you pay more just for a phone that looks and feels premium even if specs are same?
▸ Software & UI — stock Android, custom skin, bloatware, AI features?
▸ 5G connectivity — is 5G important to you for your next phone? Why or why not?

How much are you likely to spend on your next phone? What is the approximate budget range you have in mind for your next phone?
▸ Same budget range, slightly higher, or significantly higher?
▸ What would justify spending more — specific features, brand, status?
▸ Would it come from personal savings, or would someone else contribute?

What would push you to go 10,000–15,000 PKR above that? What feature or brand would justify it?
▸ If the phone you wanted was PKR 10,000 above your budget — what would you do?
Wait and save? Opt for installments / easy monthly installments? Buy a slightly lower model?

- Which models of mobile phones are mainly considering for future purchase? How did this list of models come to your attention?
Is [Infinix / Samsung / Vivo] on that list? Why or why not? What are the reasons?

do not suggest categories. Note exact first response.
